McMaster University (commonly referred McMaster Mac) public research university Hamilton, Ontario, Canada. The main McMaster campus 121 hectares (300 acres) land near the residential neighbourhoods Ainslie Wood and Westdale, adjacent the Royal Botanical Gardens. operates six academic faculties: the DeGroote School Business, Engineering, Health Sciences, Humanities, Social Science, and Science. member the U15, group universities Canada.
ACU, ATS, AUCC, CARL, COU, CUP, Fields Institute, IAU, U15.
The university bears the name William McMaster, prominent Canadian senator and banker who bequeathed C$900,000 its founding. was incorporated under the terms act the Legislative Assembly Ontario 1887, merging the Toronto Baptist College with Woodstock College. opened Toronto 1890. Inadequate facilities and the gift land Hamilton prompted its relocation 1930. The Baptist Convention Ontario and Quebec controlled the university until became privately chartered, publicly funded institution 1957.
McMaster University co-educational, and has over 25,000 undergraduate and over 4,000 post-graduate students. Alumni and former students reside across Canada and 139 countries. Its athletic teams are known the Marauders, and are members Sports. Notable alumni include government officials, academics, business leaders, Rhodes Scholars, Gates Cambridge Scholars, and Nobel laureates.
the university grew, McMaster Hall started become overcrowded. The suggestion move the university Hamilton was first brought student and Hamilton native 1909, although the proposal was not seriously considered the university until two years later. the 1920s, after previous proposals between various university staff, the Hamilton Chamber Commerce launched campaign bring McMaster University Hamilton. the issue space McMaster Hall became more acute, the university administration debated the future the university. The university nearly became federated with the University Toronto, had been the case with Trinity College and Victoria College.
Instead, 1927, the university administration decided move the university Hamilton. The Baptist Convention Ontario and Quebec secured $1.5 million, while the citizens Hamilton raised additional $500,000 help finance the move. The lands for the university and new buildings were secured through gifts from graduates. Lands were transferred from Royal Botanical Gardens establish the campus area. The first academic session the new Hamilton campus began 1930. McMaster's property Toronto was sold the University Toronto when McMaster moved Hamilton 1930. McMaster Hall now home the Royal Conservatory Music.
Hamilton Hall was constructed 1926 preparation for the university's move Hamilton.
McMaster's oldest buildings are examples Collegiate Gothic architecture, with architectural elements such carved ornamentation, bas-reliefs, recessed arched entryways, and ashlar found throughout these buildings.
McMaster University the city Hamilton, Ontario, the Golden Horseshoe along the western end Lake Ontario.
